dateline: Johnson Space Center, Houston / Kennedy Space Center — 2017–Present
heroHeadline: Artemis Accords Reach 63 Nations; Orion Heat Shield Inspection Week 2 Continues
heroSubtitle: Apr 23: Jordan signed the Artemis Accords as the 63rd signatory at NASA HQ — the second new signatory in three days, after Latvia joined Apr 20 as the 62nd, reflecting diplomatic momentum from Artemis II's April 1–10 crewed lunar flyby. At KSC, the formal 30-day Orion heat shield inspection continues in week two at the Multi-Payload Processing Facility; results expected ~May 10 will define Artemis IV's 2028 crewed lunar landing timeline. Blue Origin's New Glenn was grounded by the FAA Apr 19 after an upper stage failure on its third launch — a setback for the Artemis V HLS provider.
footerNote: Data sourced from NASA.gov, OIG Reports, SpaceNews, Ars Technica, Congressional appropriations. Tier 1: NASA/ESA/Official. Tier 2: SpaceNews/Ars Technica/Reuters. Tier 3: Planetary Society/AIAA.
